tailwinds.relevance: 37-gate consolidated hub with modern ramp and baggage infrastructure gives a clean, standardized airside environment for autonomous baggage tractor routes; Delta's Unifi stake links carrier and handler decision-making.

operationsNotes: Mixed GSE model: Delta self-handles its Terminal C hub with its own ramp agents (gvwire, Apr 2025), Unifi provides contracted ramp/cabin labor at LGA (ZipRecruiter postings, 2026), and Swissport handles third-party carriers including Air Canada and Spirit (swissport.com LGA station page; Aviation Pros, 2023). Terminal C has a rebuilt baggage system with 3+ miles of conveyor across 37 gates.
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the LGA ramp (searches for AeroVect, TractEasy, Aurrigo, UISEE and generic airside autonomy trials at LGA returned nothing). Attractive: brand-new terminals (Terminal B 2022, Delta Terminal C fully complete end of 2024), 164-port eGSE charging buildout at Terminal B by 2027 creating a fleet-refresh window, Unifi presence (Delta minority owner) as a handler-led entry path, and high NYC ramp labor cost and union pressure strengthening the retrofit ROI case. Watch factor: LGA is a dense, space-constrained short-haul ramp with no known Port Authority autonomy program.

name: Cairo International Airport

groundHandlers.name: EgyptAir Ground Services
groundHandlers.role: Dominant ramp and passenger handler at CAI; subsidiary of state-owned EgyptAir Holding, handles home carrier plus third-party airlines
groundHandlers.notes: Hosted the 38th IATA Ground Handling Conference in Cairo, May 2026. Source: https://flock.aero/companies/1303-egyptair-ground-services and https://www.iata.org/en/pressroom/2026-releases/2026-04-15-01/

groundHandlers.name: Egyptian Aviation Services (EAS)
groundHandlers.role: Second ground handler; ramp, passenger, cargo and lounge services at major Egyptian airports incl. Cairo
groundHandlers.notes: Established 1989 by the Egyptian government. Source: https://eas-eg.com/

groundHandlers.name: Aero Services Egypt (ASE)
groundHandlers.role: ISAGO-certified handler with main station at Cairo International
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://www.asegrp.aero/locations/Cairo-International-Airport

groundHandlers.name: ZAS Aviation Services
groundHandlers.role: Executive/general aviation and supervisory handling at CAI
groundHandlers.notes: Source: https://www.zasair.com/

tailwinds.headline: Egypt unveils $3.5B aviation overhaul: Cairo Terminal 4 to lift capacity to ~30M pax plus new 1M-ton cargo city
tailwinds.relevance: Major capacity growth means more turnarounds and more tug movements on the CAI ramp; modernization budget creates an opening for new-generation GSE

tailwinds.headline: Cairo hosted the 38th IATA Ground Handling Conference, themed 'Adapting Ground Operations in an Era of AI', hosted by EgyptAir
tailwinds.relevance: Puts ground-ops automation and AI on the agenda of the exact CAI stakeholders (EgyptAir Ground Services, Ministry of Civil Aviation) AeroVect would sell to

tailwinds.headline: EgyptAir Ground Services to convert ground handling equipment to electric power by 2028
tailwinds.relevance: Fleet-wide GSE replacement cycle is the natural moment to add retrofit autonomy; signals the dominant handler is actively investing in its tractor fleet

operationsNotes: CAI is Egypt's largest hub. Handling is handler_led: EgyptAir Ground Services (state airline subsidiary acting as the dominant third-party handler), EAS, ASE and ZAS provide ramp services and own the GSE; foreign carriers buy handling rather than running their own ramps. Separate IFC-advised PPP program covers 11 other Egyptian airports (Hurghada first), not CAI (https://www.ifc.org/en/pressroom/2025/egypt-partners-with-ifc-to-introduce-public-private-partnerships-at-11-airports).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the CAI ramp, and the modernization push (electric GSE by 2028, Terminal 4, IGHC 2026 AI theme) is real. But handling is dominated by state-owned EgyptAir Ground Services and EAS, procurement is government-led, and Egypt's low ramp labor costs weaken the RaaS labor-substitution ROI. Interesting to track via the electric-GSE conversion program; not an obvious near-term prospect.

name: Vienna International Airport

groundHandlers.name: Vienna Airport Handling Services (Flughafen Wien AG division)
groundHandlers.role: Dominant full-service handler: ramp, passenger, cargo, load control, de-icing (10 Safeaero + 5 Vestergaard Elephant BETA rigs)
groundHandlers.notes: In-house handling division of the airport operator itself. Source: https://www.viennaairport.com/en/business__partner/aviation__handling/ground_handling

groundHandlers.name: Celebi Ground Services Austria GmbH
groundHandlers.role: Second licensed handler: full passenger and ramp handling (loading/unloading, pushback/towing, crew transport, cleaning); also operates the boarding bridges at VIE
groundHandlers.notes: Operating at VIE since 2011; only non-airport company offering complete passenger plus ramp service. Sources: http://www.celebihandling.at/en/corporate and https://www.celebiaviation.com/about

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.role: Cargo warehouse operations only (two air cargo warehouses), not ramp handling
groundHandlers.notes: At VIE since 2008. Source: https://www.swissport.com/en/network/europe/austria/vie

tailwinds.headline: Vienna Airport caps record-breaking 2025 with 32-millionth passenger (full-year 32.6M)
tailwinds.relevance: Record ramp volumes stress baggage/cargo tug capacity and strengthen the throughput case for autonomous tractor retrofits.

tailwinds.headline: Record EUR 330M infrastructure investment for 2026 incl. Terminal 3 southern expansion (70,000 m2, opening Q2 2027) alongside strict cost-cutting on personnel expenses
tailwinds.relevance: Expansion adds gates and tow distances while management explicitly targets personnel cost reduction, a direct opening for RaaS autonomy on existing tractors. Expansion detail: https://viennaairport.com/en/business__partner/infrastructure/suederweiterung

tailwinds.headline: Austrian airport/ground-handling collective agreement adds +3.3% wages from 1 May 2025, after +7.0% in May 2024, for 6,500 workers
tailwinds.relevance: Compounding double-digit ramp labor cost inflation over two years improves the ROI math for autonomous GSE.

operationsNotes: GSE is handler_led: Vienna Airport Handling Services is a division of the airport operator Flughafen Wien AG and provides most ramp/baggage handling (https://www.viennaairport.com/en/business__partner/aviation__handling/ground_handling); Celebi Ground Services Austria is the second full ramp handler since 2011 and also runs the boarding bridges (http://www.celebihandling.at/en/corporate). Labor: no VIE ground-staff strikes found (2024 Austrian Airlines strikes were airline crew, not ramp), but sector-wide Austrian airport/ground-handling collective agreements delivered +7.0% wages effective 1 May 2024 for 6,500 workers (https://www.gpa.at/kollektivvertrag/transport-und-verkehr/oeffentliche-flughaefen/kollektivvertrag-2024-fuer-oeffentliche-flughaefen) and +3.3% effective 1 May 2025 (https://www.vida.at/de/artikel/luft-und-schiffsverkehr/2025/flughafen-kv-2025); airport management is running a strict cost-cutting program on personnel expenses for 2026 (https://aviation.direct/en/Vienna-Airport:-record-year-2025-and-outlook-for-a-challenging-business-year-2026).
aerovectNotes: No autonomous-GSE vendor found on the VIE ramp (searches for EasyMile/TractEasy, Aurrigo, UISEE and generic autonomous baggage tractor activity at VIE returned nothing). Ramp is handler_led with the airport operator's own division as dominant handler, so one buyer controls most of the tractor fleet. Entry logic: management announced a strict cost-cutting program on operating and personnel expenses for 2026 while ramp wages compounded +7.0% (May 2024) and +3.3% (May 2025) under the national airport KV; an opex-based retrofit RaaS pitch fits that squeeze. Celebi (a known major handler globally) is a second entry path. Automation appetite is demonstrated terminal-side (autonomous wheelchair PoC citing staff shortage, per https://cities-today.com/vienna-airport-set-to-trial-autonomous-wheelchairs/) and the airport already runs ~400 electric GSE units. Caveat: 2026 traffic is guided down to ~30M pax on Ryanair/Wizz capacity cuts, which may slow discretionary projects.

name: Palma de Mallorca Airport

groundHandlers.name: Groundforce (Globalia)
groundHandlers.role: Third-party ramp handler, one of three 7-year Aena licenses 2024-2031
groundHandlers.notes: Retained PMI in the Sept 2023 Aena tender; incumbent Iberia lost Mallorca. Sources: preferente.com and eleconomista.es tender coverage.

groundHandlers.name: Menzies Aviation
groundHandlers.role: Third-party ramp and passenger handler, Aena license 2024-2031
groundHandlers.notes: Won PMI in the ~EUR 5B Aena tender; committed to brand-new electric GSE with telematics. Handles easyJet, BA, Turkish, Norwegian, Wizz per local press. Source: menziesaviation.com.

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.notes: New entrant at PMI in 2024 with ~300 staff and a fleet reported ~64.9% electric. Source: swissport.com PMI launch release (found via search; page blocked direct fetch).

groundHandlers.name: Azul Handling (Ryanair Group)
groundHandlers.notes: Ryanair's in-house Spanish handler operates at PMI; center of the Aug-Dec 2025 strike wave. Source: palmaairport.info.

tailwinds.headline: Palma Airport enters final stretch of EUR 560M redevelopment, completion targeted summer 2026
tailwinds.relevance: Major modernization program with carbon-neutrality goals and 65 concurrent projects signals appetite for new airside technology and updated infrastructure that autonomous tractors can slot into.

tailwinds.headline: PMI closes 2025 with record 33.8M passengers and 246,486 movements
tailwinds.relevance: Record, heavily seasonal volumes on a strike-prone ramp increase baggage-flow pressure and the ROI of autonomy that absorbs summer peaks without added headcount.

tailwinds.headline: Swissport commits ~EUR 1.5B over five years to electric GSE and fleet electrification
tailwinds.relevance: Swissport holds one of PMI's three ramp licenses; a global fleet-renewal cycle creates a natural window to add retrofit autonomy on new electric tractors.

operationsNotes: Ramp is handler-led for third-party work (Groundforce, Menzies, Swissport own their GSE under Aena licenses) with carrier self-handling by Ryanair via Azul Handling, so model is mixed. Azul Handling ran rolling strikes at PMI and 11 other Spanish airports from 2025-08-15 through 2025-12-31 (Wed/Fri/Sat/Sun stoppages) over pay parity, guaranteed hours, and job insecurity; Menzies PMI staff (UGT) threatened Aug 2025 strikes, suspended after a draft deal on shifts, hours, and progression. Highly seasonal traffic peaks (Germany and UK markets ~15.8M pax combined) strain summer ramp staffing.
aerovectNotes: Spain's third-busiest airport (33.8M pax 2025, record) with no autonomous-GSE vendor found on the ramp. Three fresh 7-year handler licenses (2024-2031) came with electric-GSE investment commitments, so fleets are new and telematics-equipped, a good retrofit target. High seasonality plus sustained handling strikes (Azul Aug-Dec 2025, Menzies near-strike Aug 2025) strengthen the automation case. Constraint to plan for: handling access is governed by Aena's centralized license regime, so entry likely runs through Menzies, Swissport, or Groundforce corporate rather than the airport.

name: Zürich Airport

groundHandlers.name: Swissport
groundHandlers.notes: Zurich is Swissport’s home market; Swissport is the world’s #1 handler

aerovectNotes: Swissport (world #1 handler) is running its first global autonomous ground-handling pilot here with Aurrigo (announced May 2025, simulation phase first, live Auto-DollyTug trial as the next phase). Contested, not settled: retrofit-as-a-service is the counter-position if the pilot underdelivers.

competitors.status: live trial (pilot)
competitors.source: Swissport-Aurrigo ZRH pilot announced May 2025, starting with the Auto-Sim digital simulation phase; Aurrigo (Mar 2026) lists Zurich among eight live trials, not commercial deployments. https://www.airport-suppliers.com/aurrigo-international-brings-auto-dolly-tug-and-auto-shuttle-to-pte-world-2026/

tailwinds.headline: Swissport and Aurrigo launch first global pilot of autonomous ground handling at ZRH
tailwinds.relevance: Signals handler appetite for autonomy; the account is winnable if Aurrigo underdelivers
